feed.title: Bitcoin Core v30.3rc1 is now available

entry.title: Bitcoin Core v30.3rc1 is now available
entry.summary:
The latest release candidate for Bitcoin Core, version v30.3rc1, is now available for download and testing. This minor version release candidate can be accessed with binaries from [Bitcoin Core's official site](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-30.3/test.rc1/), while the source code is hosted on GitHub under a signed tag which can be viewed [here](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/tree/v30.3rc1). Release candidates such as this serve as test versions prior to the final release, allowing users to identify and report any critical issues.
Detailed preliminary notes pertaining to this release are also available and can be found in the documentation section on GitHub at [release notes](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/v30.3rc1/doc/release-notes.md). These notes provide essential information that helps users understand the changes and improvements made in this version. Should testers encounter any problems during their evaluation of the release candidate, they are encouraged to report these issues via the project’s GitHub issues page, accessible [here](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues).
This notification was distributed to subscribers of the "Bitcoin Development Mailing List" group on Google Groups, indicating ongoing communication and updates provided to developers and interested parties within the community regarding new releases and updates on the Bitcoin Core project.
